app.title: XClearp
app.subtitle: Cross-Platform System Cleaner
settings.title: System Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your preferences, startup behavior, and cleaning rule library here.
settings.general.title: General Configuration
settings.general.subtitle: Control program startup and core cleaning behavior
settings.general.startup.desc: Automatically start XClearp when you log into your system
settings.general.minToTray.desc: Hide to system tray instead of quitting when clicking close (X) button
settings.general.notifyOnComplete.desc: Send system notifications when scanning and cleaning tasks complete
settings.general.deepScan.desc: Perform thorough directory traversal to find hidden cache residues
settings.general.safeMode.desc: Move files to Trash instead of permanent deletion. Applies to scan cleanup, disk analysis deletion, and app uninstall. Files can be recovered from Trash if deleted by mistake.
settings.general.theme.desc: Switch the appearance style of the interface
settings.general.language.desc: Choose the display language of the application
settings.permissions.title: System Disk Access Permissions
settings.permissions.desc: Due to OS sandboxing and permission protection, cleaning some system caches requires additional authorization.
settings.permissions.macos.title: 💡 How to Grant Full Disk Access on macOS?
settings.permissions.macos.step1: Click the button below to open Privacy & Security settings.
settings.permissions.macos.step2: Find "Full Disk Access" in the list.
settings.permissions.macos.step3: Enable the switch for XClearp. If XClearp is not in the list, click the + button in the lower left corner to add it.
settings.permissions.macos.step4: Restart the application after configuration to take effect.
settings.permissions.windows.title: 💡 How to Run as Administrator?
settings.permissions.windows.desc1: Close the current program, right-click on the XClearp shortcut or xclearp.exe file, and select "Run as administrator".
settings.permissions.windows.desc2: To always run as administrator by default: right-click properties → Compatibility → check "Run this program as an administrator", then click OK.
settings.rules.title: Cleaning Rules Library
settings.rules.subtitle: Configure and manage your file cleaning groups and their matching rules
settings.rules.noRules: No rules yet, click the button below to create one
settings.group.create.title: Create New Rule Group
settings.group.name: Group Name
settings.group.description.placeholder: Describe the target and scope of this group...
settings.about.title: XClearp System Utility
settings.about.desc: Cross-platform desktop system cleaning utility. Built with Tauri + React, dedicated to providing fast, low-memory, and safe system cache and junk cleaning solutions.
settings.about.copyright: © 2026 XClearp Authors. Released under the MIT License.
delete.rule.title: Confirm Delete Rule
delete.rule.message: Are you sure you want to delete this cleaning rule?
delete.rule.warning: This operation will permanently remove this rule from your custom cleaning rules and cannot be undone.
delete.group.title: Confirm Delete Rule Group
delete.group.message: Are you sure you want to delete this rule group?
delete.group.warning: All custom rules under this group will also be physically deleted. This operation cannot be undone.
toast.import.format: Incorrect rule JSON format, expected an array or object of rules
scan.subtitle: Quickly scan and safely clean system cache, residual junk files, and invalid temporary files.
scan.list.title: System Clean List
scan.folder.more_items: {count} more items not displayed... (Click to expand)
scan.state.ready.desc: Click the icon above or the 'Start Scan' button to search for cleanable redundant files defined by rule groups.
scan.confirm.clean_all: Are you sure you want to clean all {count} discovered junk files (approx. {size})? This action cannot be undone.
uninstall.subtitle: Select an application to scan and delete all its residual files (cache, preferences, logs, etc.).
uninstall.review.title: {app} Residual Files
uninstall.official.desc: Please follow the instructions of the application's official uninstaller to complete the basic uninstall process. Once done, click the button below to start the deep residue cleanup.
uninstall.official.completed: I have completed the official uninstall
uninstall.state.deleting_desc: Safely deleting system residues and registry keys...
uninstall.completed.title: Uninstall Completed!
uninstall.completed.desc: Successfully uninstalled the application and cleaned all selected residual junk.
uninstall.confirm.title: Confirm Delete
uninstall.confirm.message: Are you sure you want to permanently delete {app} and its selected {size} residual junk files? This action cannot be undone!
uninstall.confirm.dialog.title: Confirm Uninstall
uninstall.confirm.dialog.desc: This will move the application to the Trash. You can also choose whether to delete the selected residual files.
reset_app_desc: Only clear cache and config files, keep the app
uninstall.state.no_residuals: No residual files found, but you can move the application itself to the Trash.
uninstall.state.official_completed: Official uninstall completed, cleaning residual files...
uninstall_partial: Some files could not be removed
disk.table.header.name: Name
disk.usage.title: Disk Usage
disk.error.junction_title: Why are some paths denied access?
disk.error.junction_desc1: On Windows systems, paths like Documents and Settings or System Volume Information are system-protected junction points or core system data zones.
disk.error.junction_desc2: These paths are created by the system to maintain compatibility with legacy software. To prevent infinite recursive scanning or accidental deletion, Windows denies read access to them by default. This is normal behavior, and you do not need to worry.
disk.error.junction_desc3: To scan other restricted regular files, try running this program as an Administrator, or grant Full Disk Access in Settings.
disk.table.whitelisted_tip: This path is in the global whitelist
disk.confirm.delete_message: Are you sure you want to delete the following path?
disk.confirm.delete_dir_warning: This action will delete this directory and all its contents. This cannot be undone.
whitelist.title: Global Whitelist
whitelist.desc: Configured paths and wildcard rules will never be scanned or cleaned, protecting your critical projects and private data.
whitelist.tip.title: Matching Rule Tips:
whitelist.tip.desc: The whitelist supports standard Glob pattern matching. For example: **/.git/** automatically ignores Git repositories in any subdirectory; **/node_modules/** protects dependency caches; *.key filters all files ending with .key.
whitelist.placeholder: Enter path or wildcard pattern to exclude globally, e.g., **/my_documents/**
whitelist.empty: No global whitelist paths. Click 'Add Path' above to add a rule.
whitelist.tooltip.disk_marker: Whitelist marker: display and mark during disk analysis scanning
whitelist.tooltip.disk_exclude: Fully exclude: do not scan this path during disk analysis
whitelist.toast.import_error_format: Invalid whitelist JSON format, expected an object
whitelist.toast.exists: This path is already in the global whitelist.
whitelist.error.add: Failed to add, unable to update whitelist
whitelist.error.delete: Failed to delete, unable to update whitelist
whitelist.error.toggle: Failed to toggle, unable to update whitelist
whitelist.error.eye: Failed to toggle visibility status, unable to update whitelist
